rationale: All three of my round-1 concrete_changes_required items are empirically ADDRESSED. (1) Idempotency: ran TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanedSpecBranch_RetryConverges in the worktree at 6cf718c9 -- PASS. It reproduces my exact repro shape (chore branch already pushed once, retry must land a fresh differing commit) via a mid-run failure injected AFTER both pushes (spec-worktree removal), converging on retry with a replaced remote tip. I independently re-probed the new RemoteHeadSHA+PushBranchForceWithLease mechanism in a fresh scratch bare-remote fixture with a genuine race: pinned the lease to a SHA observed before a third party pushed to the same branch -- git correctly rejected with '! [rejected] ... (stale info)', confirming the lease is not just idempotent but safety-preserving against concurrent writers, exactly as RemoteHeadSHA/PushBranchForceWithLease's docs claim. (2) UX: implApproveTail now gates the old spec-branch PR block on FinalizeBranch==""; TestImplApproveTail_FinalizeBranchComposition (new, in cmd/mindspec) pins both branches of the mutual exclusion -- PASS. (3) Test hygiene: TestFetchRemoteBranch_RunsNarrowFetch, TestRemoteHeadSHA_ParsesLsRemote, TestPushBranchForceWithLease_ArgvAndLease (gitutil) and TestFinalizeBranch/TestFinalizeWorktreeName/TestFinalizeWorktreePath(+custom-root) (workspace) all exist and PASS, matching the sibling-precedent convention I asked for. Full internal/executor, internal/gitutil, internal/workspace, internal/approve, cmd/mindspec suites plus go vet/go build are all green. Deviation A is a faithful pin: I confirmed FinalizeEpic's first line is `if !gitutil.BranchExists(specBranch) { return ... }`, so a literal successful-run replay (which deletes specBranch in cleanup) would hit that pre-existing, out-of-scope gate on a second call -- the injected mid-run failure (naming a real 'worktree/branch cleanup' step, exactly the retry trigger described in the consolidated Group-1 ask) is the correct way to keep specBranch alive for a genuine retry scenario. One pre-existing, already-deferred item remains untouched (CommitCount/DiffStat computed against local, possibly-stale main) but the round-1 consolidation explicitly scoped that out as a minor deferred follow-up, not a round-2 blocker.

findings.description: gitutil.RemoteHeadSHA (git ls-remote --heads, remote-authoritative) + gitutil.PushBranchForceWithLease (lease pinned to the observed SHA) replace the plain non-force push in finalizeOrphanedSpecBranch; absent-remote-branch path still uses a plain push. Leftover temp worktree self-heals (WorktreeRemoveForce -> os.RemoveAll fallback -> WorktreePrune) BEFORE any branch op.
findings.evidence: go test -count=1 ./internal/executor/ -run TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanedSpecBranch_RetryConverges -v: PASS (0.66-0.89s across runs). Also ran TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanedSpecBranch_LeftoverTempWorktreeSelfHeals: PASS. Independently reproduced in a fresh scratch bare-remote fixture (own git commands mirroring RemoteHeadSHA+PushBranchForceWithLease): (a) two sequential machine push cycles for the same branch converge without the old non-fast-forward rejection; (b) pinning the lease to a SHA observed BEFORE a third party (simulated human clone) pushed a divergent commit to the same branch correctly REJECTS with '! [rejected] chore/finalize-x -> chore/finalize-x (stale info)' -- the lease is not merely idempotent-with-self, it also protects against concurrent third-party writers, which is the exact safety property PushBranchForceWithLease's own doc comment claims.

findings.description: cmd/mindspec/impl.go's old 'Branch pushed to remote. Create a PR to merge into main' block is now gated on `result.Pushed && result.FinalizeBranch == ""`, mutually exclusive with the new NOTE block (`result.FinalizeBranch != ""`). A dedicated composition test asserts both branches: normal case prints ONLY the spec-branch PR instruction and never the NOTE; orphan case prints ONLY the NOTE (naming the chore branch) and never offers `gh pr create --head spec/...`.
findings.evidence: go test -count=1 ./cmd/mindspec/ -run TestImplApproveTail_FinalizeBranchComposition -v: PASS, both subtests. Source read of cmd/mindspec/impl.go lines ~149-165 confirms the gate and the mutually-exclusive block structure. Residual, already-deferred sub-point (not part of Group 2's must-fix scope per consolidated-round-1.md's Deferred list): result.CommitCount/DiffStat above this block are still computed against local (possibly stale) main rather than origin/main in the orphan case -- unchanged in this diff, correctly left out of round 2's scope.

findings.item: 3. Test hygiene: direct unit tests for FetchRemoteBranch + workspace helpers (my round-1 #3)
findings.description: New direct unit tests exist for FetchRemoteBranch (which shipped untested in round 1) and for the workspace naming helpers, matching the sibling-precedent convention (TestFetchRemote_RunsFetch, TestSpecWorktreeName) I cited.
findings.evidence: go test -count=1 ./internal/gitutil/ -run 'TestFetchRemoteBranch_RunsNarrowFetch|TestRemoteHeadSHA_ParsesLsRemote|TestPushBranchForceWithLease_ArgvAndLease' -v: all PASS. go test -count=1 ./internal/workspace/ -run 'TestFinalizeBranch|TestFinalizeWorktreeName|TestFinalizeWorktreePath' -v: all PASS (4 tests incl. custom-worktree-root variant). Confirmed via `git show 6f558bd6:internal/gitutil/gitops.go` that FetchRemoteBranch existed in round 1 without a direct test -- round 2 closes exactly that gap.

findings.item: Deviation A: retry test's injected-failure shape vs a literal replay
findings.description: Confirmed by reading FinalizeEpic's entry gate (`if !gitutil.BranchExists(specBranch) { return result, fmt.Errorf("spec branch %s does not exist", specBranch) }`, mindspec_executor.go line ~352) that a literal replay of a fully-successful run would immediately fail on this pre-existing, wu7t-unrelated gate on the second call, since the first run's cleanup block deletes specBranch locally. The test instead injects a failure at the spec-worktree-removal step in the cleanup block, which runs strictly AFTER both the spec-branch push and the chore-branch push (confirmed by source order: 'Push to remote' block precedes the 'Run from repo root for cleanup operations' block). This exactly matches the retry trigger named in the consolidated Group-1 ask ('a later step failed... e.g. worktree/branch cleanup') and preserves specBranch for a genuine second call, so the retry test exercises the real hazard rather than a synthetic stand-in.
findings.evidence: Source read of internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go: entry gate at line ~352-353; 'Push to remote' block at ~429-486; cleanup block (spec-worktree removal via g.WorktreeOps.Remove) at ~514-521, after the push block. finalize_orphan_test.go's TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanedSpecBranch_RetryConverges injects fake.removeErr targeting exactly that removal call.
